data.title: Nevis development project by crypto millionaire offers residents $100/month if approved, facing local backlash: FT.
data.description: Olivier Janssens has proposed a project called Destiny, offering Nevis residents $100 monthly if the government greenlights the development. The initiative has sparked backlash from locals, as per the Financial Times.

data.title: Premier Fence partners with SecurityWorks in a strategic move.
data.description: A recent report shows that unemployment rates in the US have reached 5.2%, rising slightly from the previous month. This uptick represents approximately 8.4 million Americans who are currently jobless. Additionally, the number of new job openings decreased by 105,000, totaling 10.4 million available jobs. The sectors most affected by these changes include leisure and hospitality, professional and business services, and retail trade. Experts predict a slow but steady recovery in the labor market in the coming months.

TRUMP SAYS U.S. MAY ESCORT SHIPS THROUGH HORMUZ IF NEEDED Donald Trump told Fox News Radio that the U.S. would escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary, but expressed hope that tensions ease.

data.title: Childhelp Launches "15 Seconds to Safety" Campaign with Support from Claire Holt, Dolph Lundgren, Mena Suvari, and Other Advocates
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona on March 13, 2026, it was reported that a child abuse report is made every 15 seconds in the United States. This alarming statistic emphasizes the critical need for immediate action to address child abuse. Childhelp, a national nonprofit organization focusing on the prevention and treatment of child abuse, is at the forefront of raising awareness and combating this issue.

data.title: S&P 500 Ulta Beauty Earnings Are Ugly But This Is Why Analysts Remain Positive
data.description: On Friday, Ulta Beauty saw a decline in its shares despite analysts holding a positive outlook on the cosmetics company. Despite the unimpressive earnings reported for S&P 500 Ulta Beauty, experts remain bullish on the company's future prospects.

data.title: Boeing Contracts Get $2.49 Bil. Update; KC-135s Collide Over Iraq
data.description: During Operation Epic Fury, two KC-135 aircraft were involved in an in-flight incident over Iraq. Following this, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and RTX were awarded substantial contracts. Boeing contracts received a total update of $2.49 billion.

data.title: Job Openings Bounce, Inflation Hot, Keeping Fed On Hold; S&P 500 Rises (Live Coverage)
data.description: Inflation has been a continuing issue prior to the Iran conflict. Following a surge in job openings, inflation remains high, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain its current stance. Concurrently, the S&P 500 saw an increase in value.

data.title: Children's Hospital of Philadelphia's Susan C. Nicolson, MD, Honored with Congenital Cardiac Anesthesia Society Lifetime Achievement Award
data.description: Today, Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) disclosed that Dr. Susan C. Nicolson, an attending anesthesiologist, has significantly influenced the training and implementation of congenital cardiac anesthesia globally. Dr. Nicolson's groundbreaking efforts have had a profound impact on this field.

data.title: Printing Limitless Brings Professional-Grade Custom Vinyl Banner Printing to Businesses Nationwide
data.description: On March 13, 2026, in both New York and Los Angeles, a renowned company called Printing Limitless is assisting businesses nationwide in finding cost-effective methods to market their products and services. With a reputation for providing tailored printing solutions, Printing Limitless aims to support enterprises in their promotional endeavors.

data.title: OpenFold Consortium Announces Major OpenFold3 Update and Public Release of Training Data for Reproducible Biomolecular AI
data.description: In Berkeley, California, researchers are making it possible to validate and iterate quickly, allowing them to transform cofolding models into scientific infrastructure that accelerates drug discovery. This advancement aims to streamline the process and enhance efficiency in research and development efforts.

data.title: Bitcoin's Cycle Is Intact, But The Bottom May Come In 2027, Analyst Says
data.description: An expert analyst, Benjamin Cowen, stated that Bitcoin's four-year market cycle continues to align with historical patterns, contradicting claims that the cycle has been broken. Despite social media speculation, Cowen emphasized that the cycle remains consistent, with major peaks historically appearing in Q4 of post-halving years. Many investors believed the cycle failed due to Bitcoin not reaching anticipated price targets or altcoins underperforming. Cowen cautions against prematurely declaring the end of the current bear market in 2026, as the cycle's timeline still corresponds to previous cycles. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Authorities dismantle SocksEscort network in crypto scam. 34 domains, 23 servers seized; $3.5M in crypto frozen with 369,000 devices hacked.
data.description: Authorities have confiscated a total of 34 domains and 23 servers, alongside freezing approximately $3.5 million in cryptocurrency, all related to SocksEscort. This proxy service has been discovered to have taken control of a staggering 369,000 devices.
